auth.signInSubtitle: Sign in to your NebulaCloud workspace.
auth.registerSubtitle: Free for the first 10 services, no credit card required.
auth.terms: By creating an account, you agree to the Terms of Service.

landing.heroSubtitle: NebulaCloud is a self-hosted PaaS in the spirit of Railway, Render, and Heroku. Connect a repo, ship a container, get realtime logs, metrics, and a polished dashboard — all on infrastructure you control.
landing.heroFootnote: Free to self-host. No credit card. Boots on Docker Compose in under a minute.
landing.ctaSubtitle: Spin up the full stack with one make-target. Connect a repo. Press deploy. Watch logs stream in real time.
landing.footerTagline: Self-hosted PaaS for shipping containerised applications straight from Git. Built as a portfolio-grade reference architecture.

featuresSection.pushBody: Connect a GitHub repo, push to your branch, and NebulaCloud builds, ships, and rolls it out behind HTTPS.
featuresSection.buildBody: Cloud Native Buildpacks auto-detect Node, Python, Go and .NET stacks. Bring a Dockerfile if you'd rather opt out.
featuresSection.logsBody: Stream stdout from every container directly into your dashboard, with structured fields and correlation IDs.
featuresSection.terminalBody: Open a shell inside any running container. SSH-grade ergonomics from the browser, scoped by RBAC.
featuresSection.secretsBody: Environment variables sealed with AES-256-GCM at rest. Pluggable for KMS / Vault when you need it.
featuresSection.domainsBody: Bring your own hostname, NebulaCloud provisions Let's Encrypt certificates and rotates them automatically.

featureGrid.title: Everything between {highlight} and a live URL.
featureGrid.subtitle: Build pipeline, runtime, ingress, observability — composed into a single cohesive experience instead of a stitched-together stack.

howSection.label: How it works
howSection.title: From {mono} to {live} in four steps.
howSection.connectBody: Install the GitHub App and pick the repository. Branch, environment, and runtime live in one config.
howSection.buildBody: Workers detect your stack and build a tiny OCI image with Cloud Native Buildpacks (or your Dockerfile).
howSection.deployBody: Runtime agent rolls out a new container with healthcheck and zero-downtime cutover behind Traefik.
howSection.observeBody: Logs, metrics, build history, audit trail — all in the dashboard, scoped to your org and roles.

notFound.title: That page doesn't exist.
notFound.description: The route you tried to reach has drifted off the manifest. Head back to your dashboard or jump to the project list.

overview.title: Overview
overview.subtitle: Workspace snapshot — live counts from the API where noted; charts are illustrative only.
overview.mvpText: project and service totals come from your organization. Recent deployments list uses the first project only until a workspace-wide feed exists. Sparkline charts are not backed by metrics APIs.
overview.requestDesc: Illustrative only — no org-wide metrics API yet.
overview.cpuDesc: Illustrative only — use project metrics for API-backed samples.
overview.timelineEmpty: Create a project to see deployment history here.

deployments.title: Deployments
deployments.subtitleFilter: Audit releases in this workspace (single service filter)
deployments.subtitleDefault: Audit releases in this workspace (first project)

domains.title: Domains
domains.subtitle: Bring your own hostname and TLS — planned for Phase 7 (not available in this MVP).
domains.cardDesc: The control plane does not expose domain APIs yet. The previous table used static mock data; it has been removed so the dashboard stays truthful.
domains.cardBody: See repository README.md Phase 7 (custom domains + ACME). Until then, services use the default hostname produced by the runtime agent (service.project.base).

metrics.title: Metrics
metrics.subtitle: Org-wide metrics are not available in the MVP — open a project for API-backed samples.
metrics.noData: No datapoints yet — once Prometheus scrape labels match this service id, graphs appear here.
metrics.noService: Create a service in this project to view scaffolded charts.
metrics.memoryDesc: Resident set size, p95 across replicas
metrics.noSeriesReturned: No series returned yet — the API returns a scaffold until Prometheus is fully wired.
metrics.orgNoServices: No services yet. Create a project, add a service, then return here — or open Project → Metrics from the sidebar.

projects.title: Projects
projects.subtitle: Group services that share a repo, secrets, and team.
projects.emptyDesc: Select an organization above, or create one. Then add a project and use <connect>Connect repository</connect> from the project header to link a GitHub repo and webhook URL.
projects.noProjectsYet: No projects yet — add a starter project above.

activity.title: Activity
activity.description: Sample timeline (not wired to the API). For real history use

connectRepo.title: Connect repository
connectRepo.description: Link a GitHub repo for automatic deploys on push. Optionally register the GitHub App installation ID.
connectRepo.installationHint: From the GitHub App install page — scopes webhooks to this project.
connectRepo.needProject: Open this dialog from a project to save the repository URL.
connectRepo.invalidInstallation: GitHub installation ID must be a positive integer.
connectRepo.savedWithInstall: Repository and GitHub installation saved. Webhooks will scope to this installation.
connectRepo.savedRepoOnly: Repository saved. Add the webhook URL in GitHub for automatic deploys on push.
connectRepo.dialogDescription: Saves the clone URL and default branch on this project. Optionally link your GitHub App installation id so webhooks with installation.id match only this project. Configure a GitHub webhook below to trigger deploys on push.
connectRepo.webhookHint: Use Content type application/json, secret from NEBULA_GITHUB_APP_WEBHOOK_SECRET, events: push.
connectRepo.installationPlaceholder: e.g. 12345678 — from the app install URL or GitHub API
connectRepo.installationHintLong: After installing the Nebula GitHub App on your org or repo, GitHub shows this numeric id in the install settings URL. Leave blank if you only match on repository URL (dev / simple setups).

detail.addServiceFirst: Add a service from the overview first.
detail.noServices: No services yet — add one above.

settings.title: Project settings
settings.subtitle: Name, slug, and repository configuration.
settings.name: Name
settings.generalDesc: Update display name, description, repository URL, and default branch. Slug stays fixed after creation.
settings.dangerDesc: Delete this project and all of its services, deployments, and audit history. API support is not implemented yet.

env.title: Environment
env.subtitle: Encrypted at rest (AES-256-GCM). Injected into containers at runtime.
env.envVarsDesc: Sealed at rest with AES-256-GCM. Scoped per service; pick a service to edit its env.
env.noVariables: No variables for this service yet.

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Manage your profile, security, API keys, and dashboard appearance.
settings.placeholderText: the sessions and API keys tables below are static demos — the API does not yet list refresh sessions or PATs. Profile and appearance reflect your signed-in user where applicable.

security.changePasswordDesc: Argon2id hashed server-side, with rotation logged in your audit trail.
security.mfaDesc: Time-based one-time passwords. Phase 9 ships hardware key support.
security.sessionsDesc: Sign out other devices if you suspect anything.

keys.title: API keys
keys.description: Use API keys for CI/CD integrations. Treat them like passwords.
keys.keyCreated: API key created. Copy it before closing.

appearance.title: Theme
appearance.description: Dark is the default — light theme is also supported.

table.title: Deployments
table.description: Every deploy across your workspace, newest first. Click a row to inspect its pipeline.

domains.cardDesc: NebulaCloud provisions Let's Encrypt certificates and renews them automatically.
domains.dialogDesc: Point your domain to NebulaCloud and we'll handle the rest. Backend lands in Phase 7.
